title: Cyclosorus dentatus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus dentatus belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns. This species is a member of the genus Cyclosorus, a group of species commonly known as the "cyclosorus ferns". The genus Cyclosorus is further classified under the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The plant is also a part of the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. At the kingdom level, Cyclosorus dentatus is classified as Plantae.
uses: Cyclosorus dentatus is a plant species known for its potential medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems. The plant's extracts have been studied for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Traditional practitioners may use the plant to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other illnesses. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cyclosorus dentatus for medicinal purposes.

intro: Cyclosorus dentatus, also known as the Toothed Cinnamon Fern or Cinnamon Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus dentatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in herbal infusions and decoctions, particularly in Asian and African folk medicine.
